behaviors.description: The generated reply references specific points from the incoming email rather than producing a generic response.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is not just local parsing; it is whether the model grounds content in thread details. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network is required to trigger this failure. MOCK-HIDE/LLM-DEP: a stubbed model would always return fixed grounded text and hide generic-response failures; correctness depends on real LLM output quality. MULTI-STEP: this behavior targets one model judgment (grounded drafting), not a multi-agent sequence, so Workflow is rejected by the behavior text: "generated reply references specific points" (single drafting outcome).
behaviors.test_description: Run the real drafting model on varied real email threads and assert the draft cites thread-specific entities/events (and fails when prompts/context are degraded), measuring grounding rate versus generic-template responses.

behaviors.description: The factual consistency check flags claims in the draft that contradict entries in the knowledge base.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug appears at module handoff (claim extraction/checker + KB lookup), not in one isolated function. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess in behavior text; local KB fixture is sufficient. (b) No explicit dependence on model output quality is stated. REJECTION-GATE (demotion below Agentic): deterministic-plumbing text: "factual consistency check flags claims ... contradict entries in the knowledge base"; exclusion text: behavior describes contradiction against KB entries, not "model decides if true." Thus Integration is minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Wire draft-claim parser, KB retriever, and contradiction detector together with a local KB dataset; inject contradictory and non-contradictory claims and verify flags propagate correctly to the review result.

behaviors.description: When the knowledge base has no relevant entries, the draft includes a disclaimer rather than fabricating facts.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is not only retrieval plumbing; the key failure is fabricated vs abstaining language in generated text. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network needed. MOCK-HIDE/LLM-DEP: mocked generation can force disclaimer and miss hallucination failures; correctness depends on real model behavior under missing evidence. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow rejected because behavior specifies one output decision in draft generation after "no relevant entries," not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Use real model calls with prompts/context where retrieval returns empty evidence; assert drafts contain required disclaimer patterns and do not introduce unsupported factual claims across repeated runs.

behaviors.description: The approval workflow blocks sending until the user explicitly approves — no draft is sent automatically.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at state handoff between draft state, approval state, and send action. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) behavior does not require real network/send provider to detect premature send; a local sender stub catches it. (b) No dependence on model output content. (c) No multi-agentic chain. REJECTION-GATE (demotion below Agentic): deterministic-plumbing text: "approval workflow blocks sending until the user explicitly approves"; exclusion text: "no draft is sent automatically" is pure gating/orchestration, independent of LLM judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Execute workflow modules end-to-end in-process: create draft, attempt send before approval (must be blocked), then apply explicit approval and verify send is invoked exactly once.

behaviors.description: The full sequence — read thread, generate draft, check facts, present for approval — works end-to-end for a real email thread.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires a full pipeline with sequential stages and at least one agentic decision (draft generation), likely plus additional decisioning in fact-check handling. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing stage outputs can hide cross-stage failures in context carryover and gating order. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: even if some parts are deterministic, this is the named "full sequence" and real-thread end-to-end behavior, so lower-level tests do not cover pipeline-level failure modes. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic alone is insufficient because text requires multiple sequenced steps: "read thread, generate draft, check facts, present for approval."
behaviors.test_description: Run the production-like pipeline on a real email thread fixture through all stages (ingest -> generation -> fact-check -> approval UI state), validating stage ordering, data carryover, and final blocked-until-approval outcome.
